title: Sindhu Dominates Han Yue at Japan Open, Hints at New Format Advantage
indian_angle: India's top female shuttler PV Sindhu secured a significant win, demonstrating her potential in a new scoring format.
summary: PV Sindhu defeated world No. 5 Han Yue at the Japan Open with a score of 21-16, 21-14, with analysts suggesting her aggressive style is well-suited for the proposed 15-point format.

title: Delhi World Championships: Engaging New Generations for Badminton's Future
indian_angle: As India prepares to host the 2026 BWF World Championships in Delhi, there's a focus on attracting younger audiences to ensure the sport's long-term popularity.
summary: A discussion on the importance of drawing Gen Z and Gen Alpha audiences to badminton, especially with the upcoming BWF World Championships 2026 being held in Delhi, to secure the sport's future growth.

title: BAI President Sarma Addresses Wildlife Incidents at Indian Open
indian_angle: BAI President Himanta Biswa Sarma commented on the unusual presence of monkeys during a major Indian badminton event, providing a lighthearted perspective.
summary: BAI President Himanta Biswa Sarma responded to queries about monkeys appearing at the Indian Open, stating, 'What is the problem if monkeys come to watch a game,' highlighting a unique aspect of hosting tournaments in India.

title: New 3x15 Scoring System to Debut in Indian Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India is implementing a new 3x15 scoring system across all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships, starting in Ernakulam.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has announced the implementation of a 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships (U-13, U-15 & U-17), with its debut set for Ernakulam.

title: BAI Releases Eligibility Criteria for World Junior Championships 2026
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India has issued guidelines regarding player eligibility for the upcoming World Junior Championships 2026 in Cairo, Egypt, impacting aspiring Indian junior shuttlers.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has released a circular detailing the player eligibility requirements for the BWF World Junior Championships 2026, to be held in Cairo, Egypt.

title: All India Senior Ranking Tournament Announced for Chennai
indian_angle: The Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament 2026 is scheduled to take place in Chennai, providing a crucial platform for Indian senior players.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has announced the Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament 2026, set to be held in Chennai, Tamil Nadu, from July 16th to 23rd, 2026.
